heading: Treaty cases
label: 1502 Treaty cases
text: Except as otherwise provided by Act of Congress, the United States Court of Federal Claims shall not have jurisdiction of any claim against the United States growing out of or dependent upon any treaty entered into with foreign nations.
notes: 1948ActBased on title 28, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 259 (, ).Mar. 3, 1911, ch. 231, § 15336 Stat. 1138Phrase “Except as otherwise provided by enactment of Congress” was inserted to cover cases where special Acts confer jurisdiction. (See , 1943, 97 Ct.Cl. 613, certiorari denied 63 S.Ct. 992, 318 U.S. 789, 87 L.Ed. 1155, and , 1873, 17 Wall. 439, 443, 21 L.Ed. 696.)Sioux Tribe of Indians v. United StatesIn re United StatesWords “not pending therein on ,” were omitted as obsolete.December 1, 1862Changes in phraseology were made.

1949ActThis section, in amending , U.S.C., conforms with the provisions of act of (ch. 959, § 25, ), which affected section 153 of the Judicial Code of 1911 by striking therefrom the words “or with Indian tribes.” Such section 153 of the Judicial Code was the source of such section 1502.section 1502 of title 2860 Stat. 1056August 13, 1946

Amendments1992— substituted “United States Court of Federal Claims” for “United States Claims Court”.Pub. L. 102–5721982— substituted “United States Claims Court” for “Court of Claims”.Pub. L. 97–1641949—Act , struck out “or with Indian tribes” after “foreign nations”.May 24, 1949
